title: Malty German Bread Krapfen
description: Soft and airy German bread dough filled with a sweet and malty mixture, topped with sesame seeds.
instructions: Combine warm water, yeast, and 1 teaspoon of sugar in a small bowl. Let the mixture sit for 5 minutes, until it becomes frothy.
In a large mixing bowl, combine flour, salt, and malted milk powder.
Add the melted butter, eggs, and egg yolks to the dry ingredients and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough on a floured surface for 10 minutes, until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour, until it has doubled in size.
Punch down the dough and divide it into 8 equal pieces.
Roll each piece into a ball and then flatten it slightly into a disk shape.
Place the disks on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving about 1 inch of space between each krapfen.
Brush the tops of the krapfen with melted butter and sprinkle with sesame seeds.
Bake the krapfen for 15-20 minutes, until they are golden brown and cooked through.
Let the krapfen cool on a wire rack for 5 minutes before serving.
